Chicago Sun-Times seeks CAR reporter

September 1995
Investigative MethodsJournalism & NewsroomData & Databases

The Chicago Sun-Times is hiring an experienced computer-assisted reporting journalist with at least three years of experience in data analysis and government database research. Interested candidates should submit their resume and clips to the assistant metro editor.

The Chicago Sun-Times is looking for an experienced reporter knowledgeable in gathering and analyzing computer data and doing complex stories using government databases. Applicants should have a minimum of three years experience in computer-assisted reporting and be proficient in the use of public records. This is not an entry-level position. Those interested should send resume and clips to John Erickson, assistant metro editor, Chicago Sun-Times, 401 N.

Wabash, Chicago, Ill., 60611.
